subject: Sociology
title: Belief Systems and Worldviews
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Belief Systems and Worldviews.

hook.text: Some see suffering as karma. Others call it God's plan. Same event, completely different meanings.

buildup.text: Every religion offers a worldview — a framework explaining why things happen and how to live.

discovery.text: Peter Berger called this a sacred canopy — religion shelters people from the terror of meaninglessness.

twist.text: When the canopy cracks, people face anomie. Sudden loss of meaning can destabilize entire societies.

climax.text: Belief systems don't just comfort individuals. They provide the shared meaning society needs to function.

quiz.question: What did Peter Berger mean by a 'sacred canopy'?
options: Religion as a shield against meaninglessness
